World.title: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
World.description: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.

World.title: Barcelona on the brink of defending La Liga title after beating Getafe
World.description: Barcelona move 11 points clear of Real Madrid in the La Liga title race with five games to play.

World.title: Arsenal narrowly beat Newcastle to regain Premier League lead over Man City
World.description: Nervy Arsenal beat Newcastle 1-0 in the EPL to move three points clear of Manchester City, who hold a game in hand.

World.title: France defends abstention on UN slave trade resolution
World.description: France justified abstaining on a UN resolution recognising the slave trade as the gravest crime against humanity.

World.title: Watch: How one orangutan braved new bridge to unite his split community
World.description: The forest where the Sumatran orangutans live has been split by a road.

World.title: Trump cancels US envoys’ trip after Iran’s Araghchi leaves Pakistan
World.description: Trump later suggests that next talks will be over phone, saying 'If they want to talk, all they have to do is call!!!'

World.title: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
World.description: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

World.title: Rights groups critical as Venezuela prisoner release scheme 'coming to an end'
World.description: More than 500 political prisoners are thought to still be in jail, despite the releases since the amnesty law was brought in.

World.title: Reported US CIA agents killed in crash not authorised to operate: Mexico
World.description: Mexican government says it is seeking details after incident raises questions about US activities on its territory.

World.title: Joy and desire for change as Gaza’s Deir el-Balah holds elections
World.description: Deir el-Balah residents vote for first time since 2006 despite destruction, displacement and genocide.

World.title: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi and Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif meet in Pakistan
World.description: Iranian FM Araghchi met Pakistani PM Sharif in Islamabad as part of efforts to revive direct negotiations with the US.

World.title: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
World.description: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

World.title: Hope in Gaza as 300 couples get married at one time
World.description: The 300 couples were selected in a draw of nearly 2,000 people to be a part of the joint celebration.

World.title: US to allow Venezuelan government to cover Maduro’s lawyer fees
World.description: Defence lawyers had asked for case to be thrown out, claiming Maduro's rights were violated following US abduction.

World.title: Explosions and gunfire as armed groups launch co-ordinated attacks across Mali
World.description: Witnesses report clashes in the centre and north, in what has been described as the largest jihadist attack in years.

World.title: Mali hit by wave of coordinated attacks from armed groups

World.title: After years of avoidance, Trump to attend first White House press dinner
World.description: Professional organisations call on attendees to 'speak forcefully' at annual event, which will not feature a comedian.

World.title: African governments need to take urgent action on fertiliser shortages
World.description: Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have slowed fertiliser shipments which could put harvests at risk.

World.title: Deporting soldiers? Why immigrant veterans fear removal from the US

World.title: Arsenal 1-0 Newcastle: Premier League – as it happened

World.title: ‘Scale and coordination of Mali attacks appear unprecedented’

World.description: International flights have resumed at Tehran’s Imam Khomeini International Airport, after weeks of disruption by the war

World.title: Armed groups stage simultaneous attacks across Mali

World.title: Iran resumes commercial flights from Tehran airport

World.title: Iran’s Infowar: Lego, AI and ever tightening control

World.title: Mali attacks updates: Coordinated attacks in Bamako, Kidal and elsewhere
World.description: These were the updates about the attacks across Mali on Saturday, April 25, 2026.

World.title: Palestinians cast their ballots as Gaza holds first election in 21 years
World.description: Palestinians are voting in Gaza’s first municipal elections in 21 years. Around 70,000 people are registered to vote.

World.title: Gunmen stage simultaneous attacks across Mali, army says
World.description: Capital Bamako and several other locations hit in an apparently coordinated ⁠assault involving multiple groups.

World.title: Top Iranian and Pakistani officials meet in Islamabad
World.description: Iran’s FM Abbas Araghchi is in Islamabad for talks with Pakistani officials, as US envoys are also set to arrive.

World.title: Overnight Russian attacks on Ukraine kill several, injure dozens
World.description: Russia launched more than 600 drones and 47 missiles as it targeted eight regions in Dnipro, authorities say.

World.title: Gaza area votes in first municipal election in 20 years
World.description: Gaza officials say Deir el-Balah vote is a 'pilot', while occupied West Bank also goes to first polls since 2023 war.

World.title: Iran's foreign minister leaves Pakistan, then Trump cancels U.S. delegation's travel

World.title: Thirty-one sloths die in Florida before opening of attraction
World.description: Many died due to conditions at a warehouse they'd been shipped to, while others arrived dead, authorities say.

World.title: Heated Rivalry, The Housemaid and Fourth Wing: TikTok launches BookTok bestseller list
World.description: The inaugural top 20 is entirely made up of female authors, with Chloe Walsh appearing most frequently.

World.title: The viral manifesto of 'anti-woke' tech boss with NHS and defence contracts

World.title: 'We cried together': Trump's deportation drive forces tough decisions for couples
World.description: The spouses of illegal immigrants facing deportation must choose between separation or moving to a new country.

World.title: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
World.description: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

Politics.title: Pope Leo reiterates opposition to death penalty on same day U.S. approves firing squads
Politics.description: Pope Leo reiterated the Catholic Church's teaching that the death penalty is "inadmissible," in a video message released hours after the Justice Department said it would allow firing squads for federal executions.

Politics.title: Key figure in Mandelson vetting row will not give evidence before MPs
Politics.description: The Foreign Affairs Committee says Ian Collard will only be giving evidence in writing.

Politics.title: Relish and dread as Starmer 'shambles' hangs over Scotland and Wales elections
Politics.description: Laura Kuenssberg travels to Wales and Scotland to speak to candidates and voters ahead of May 7 polls.

Politics.title: Week in Politics: Congress and the Iran war; Trump's approval ratings
Politics.description: We look at what Congress might do as the 60-day window to vote on the war per the War Powers Act draws near, as well as what the latest polls say about President Trump's approval ratings.

Politics.title: Trump to attend White House Correspondents' Association dinner. What's it like?
Politics.description: President Trump is attending Saturday's White House Correspondents' Dinner where presidents have historically poked fun at themselves while celebrating the journalists who cover them, something Trump has not easily done in the past.

Politics.title: The rising cost of fertilizer and fuel prices is pushing some farmers to the brink
Politics.description: In the Mississippi Delta, a crucial agricultural region, farmers say their patience is wearing thin. Reeling from the effects of tariffs, they must now also navigate rising fertilizer and fuel costs.

Politics.title: DOJ wants to shield its lawyers from outside scrutiny. Critics worry about oversight
Politics.description: Critics say the proposed rule to let the DOJ step into state bar investigations could weaken one of the last independent checks on government lawyers.

Politics.title: Trump and Congress cut funding for Planned Parenthood. Can Botox keep it afloat?
Politics.description: After President Trump and Congress cut certain Medicaid funding to Planned Parenthood in last year's budget, some clinics have started offering aesthetic services, including Botox, to stay afloat.

Science.title: Where, when and how can you get involved in IOM wildlife week?
Science.description: The annual celebration of the Isle of Man's natural environment and wildlife gets under way.

Business.title: England shirt overpriced, says £40k kits collector
Business.description: Collector Les Motherby says classic England shirts are often better value than new ones.

Business.title: Rescue centre facing eviction makes funding appeal
Business.description: Redhead Rescues Animal Sanctuary in Northamptonshire wants to buy its own land.

Culture.title: 'Very funny' statue of Monty Python's Terry Jones to be unveiled
Culture.description: Sir Michael Palin says his fellow Python star Jones would have found the statue "very funny indeed".

Culture.title: Gladiator Giant claims he was axed from BBC show over girlfriend's job
Culture.description: Jamie Bigg says he wanted to publicise his relationship and was told he "wouldn't be continuing" on the show.

Culture.title: Tensions flare in heated I'm A Celebrity live final
Culture.description: Adam Thomas, Craig Charles, Mo Farah and Harry Redknapp were in the final of I'm A Celebrity... South Africa.

Culture.title: Touring is a costly struggle for bands like us. Now Harry Styles is helping
Culture.description: Ticket money from some stadium and arena gigs is helping support struggling grassroots artists and venues.

Culture.title: Loud eaters and phones nearly spoiled my cinema trip - and it's not just me
Culture.description: Some people are being disruptive in cinemas - despite routine reminders to be considerate to others.
